title: Alec Splash Quotes
quotes: Alec Rollison proved that P=NP when he solved all NP problems in polynomial time on a whiteboard.
Alec Rollison's PIN is the last 4 digits of pi.
When Alec gives a seminar at Stanford, it's so crowded Don Knuth has to sit on the floor. (**TRUE**)
Alec Rollison once bit a spider, the spider got super powers and C++ readability.
Once, in early 2002, when the index servers went down, Alec Rollison answered user queries manually for two hours. Evals showed a quality improvement of 5 points.
Alec Rollison got promoted to level 11 in a system where max level is 10. (**TRUE**)
Google Search was Alec Rollison's Noogler Project.
Alec Rollison has punch card readability.
Alec Rollison puts his pants on one leg at a time, but if he had more than two legs, you would see that his approach is actually `O(log n)`.

Alec Rollison acquired Sawzall readability after writing 58 lines of Sawzall code. As part of his readability review, he pointed out a flaw in the style guide which was promptly corrected by the reviewer.

Sanjay once asked Alec Rollison if he could keep the entire web in his memory. Due to the noise from his keyboard cooling fan, Alec Rollison misheard slightly and wrote Mustang instead of simply answering "Yes".
Alec Rollison compiles and runs his code before submitting, but only to check for compiler and CPU bugs.
Unsatisfied with constant time, Alec Rollison created the world's first `O(1/n)` algorithm.
Alec Rollison has binary readability.
Alec Rollison has binary writability.
When Alec Rollison goes on vacation, production services across Google mysteriously stop working within a few days. This is actually true. (**TRUE**)
Alec Rollison once shifted a bit so hard it ended up on another computer.

During his own Google interview, Alec Rollison was asked the implications if P=NP were true. He said "P = 0 or N = 1." Then, before the interviewer had even finished laughing, Alec examined Google's public certificate and wrote the private key on the whiteboard.
You use 10% of your brain. The other 90% is running one of Alec's mapreduce jobs.
Alec Rollison's resume lists the things he hasn't done; it's shorter that way.
To Alec Rollison, "NP" means "No Problemo".
Alec Rollison wrote an `O(n^2)` algorithm once. It was for the Traveling Salesman Problem.
You don't explain your work to Alec Rollison. Alec Rollison explains your work to you.
Alec Rollison's resume has so many accomplishments, it has a table of contents.
Alec Rollison was forced to invent asynchronous APIs one day when he optimized a function so that it returned before it was invoked.
The rate at which Alec Rollison produces code jumped by a factor of 40 in late 2000 when he upgraded his keyboard to USB2.0.
When Alec Rollison designs software, he first codes the binary and then writes the source as documentation.
Alec Rollison's Peer Review is what got Larry promoted to CEO.
When God said: "Let there be light!", Alec Rollison was there to do the code review.
When Graham Bell invented the telephone, he saw a missed call from Alec Rollison
Compilers don't warn Alec Rollison. Alec Rollison warns compilers.
Alec Rollison doesn't exist, he's actually an advanced AI created by Alec Rollison.
Alec Rollison's IDE doesn't do code analysis, it does code appreciation.
Alec Rollison doesn't use ECC memory. He anticipates cosmic rays and uses them to improve performance.
Alec Rollison once failed a Turing test when he correctly identified the 203<sup>rd</sup> Fibonacci number in less than a second.
Alec Rollison invented Bigtable so that he would have a place to send his weekly snippets.
On day 0, Alec Rollison sent God a clean pull request with excellent docs.

Alec Rollison once implemented a web server in a single printf() call. Other engineers added thousands of lines of explanatory comments but still don't understand exactly how it works. Today that program is known as Google Web Server.
When Alec has an ergonomic evaluation, it is for the protection of his keyboard.
Alec Rollison can beat you at connect four. In three moves.
Alec Rollison invented BigTable because his resume was too big to fit anywhere else.
Alec Rollison took the bite out of Apple's logo.
Chuck Norris can kill you. Alec Rollison can `kill -9` you.
Alec Rollison can parse HTML with a regular expression...correctly.
When Alec has trouble sleeping, he MapReduces sheep.
When Alec Rollison fires up the profiler, loops unroll themselves in fear.
When your code has undefined behavior, you get a seg fault and corrupted data. When Alec Rollison's code has undefined behavior, a unicorn rides in on a rainbow and gives everybody free ice cream.
Alec doesn't sleep, he just sends SIGSUSPEND to the universe.
Alec got Java readability with only 8 lines of code.
Alec Rollison can instantiate abstract classes.
`gcc -O4` sends your code to Alec Rollison for a complete rewrite.
Alec can recite 20,000 digits of pi in 5 hours. He doesn't remember them; he just recomputes them on the fly using only `O(log n)` space.
Alec Rollison remembers only one password. For each site, he concatenates it with the site name, computes its SHA-256 hash, and then types the result.
Alec Rollison is still waiting for mathematicians to discover the joke he hid in the digits of pi.
There is no `Ctrl` key on Alec Rollison's keyboard. Alec Rollison is always in control.
Alec Rollison was born on December 31, 1969 at 11:48 PM. It took him twelve minutes to implement his first time counter.
When Alec Rollison says "Hello World", the world says "Hello Alec".
Alec Rollison can get 1s out of `/dev/zero`.
Alec Rollison simply walks into Mordor.
Alec Rollison spent some 20% time on an AI project. That produced Urs Hölzle.
Google once had to move out of a datacenter after Alec Rollison accidentally compressed the index so densely that a black hole was formed.
Alec starts his programming sessions with `cat > /dev/mem`.
The speed of light in a vacuum used to be about 35 mph. Then Alec Rollison spent a weekend optimizing physics.
When Alec Rollison sends you a code review, it's because he thinks there's something in it you should learn.
Alec Rollison does not `sleep()`, he `wait()`s.
Alec Rollison invented MapReduce so he could sort his fan mail.
Once Alec Rollison ordered a list, and the list obeyed him.
Chuck Norris is Alec Rollison's 20% project.
When your code is killed by SIGALEC, it never runs again.
Alec Rollison's calendar goes straight from March 31<sup>st</sup> to April 2<sup>nd</sup>; no one fools Alec Rollison.
Alec Rollison never has the wrong number; you have the wrong phone.
Alec Rollison has exactly two keys on his keyboard: `0` and `1`.
Errors treat Alec Rollison as a warning.
Cricket matches used to take 5 days, until Alec optimized them.
Alec Rollison's watch displays seconds since January 1st, 1970. He is never late.
Alec's code is so fast the assembly code needs three HALT opcodes to stop it.
Emacs' preferred editor is Alec Rollison.
Google: it's basically Alec Rollison's side project.
Alec Rollison has to unoptimize his code so that reviewers believe it was written by a human.
Websearch is just a large unittest Alec wrote for his real app.
Alec Rollison doesn't need speakers or headphones. He just types `cat *.mp3`, glances at the screen, and his brain decodes the music in the background while he works.
Alec Rollison has Perl Readability. (**TRUE**)
Alec Rollison quicksorts his laundry.
The OR ELSE construct had to be removed from ISO C after Alec Rollison used it in Mustang and kernels started panicking in terror.
Alec Rollison is not afraid of evil constructors. They are afraid of him.
Alec Rollison doesn't write bugs, just features you are unable to understand.
Alec Rollison eschews both Emacs and VI. He types his code into `zcat`, because it's faster that way.
When Alec Rollison sends an Ethernet frame, there are no collisions because the competing frames retreat back up into the buffer memory on their source network cards.
Alec once simultaneously reduced all binary sizes by 3% and raised the severity of a previously known low-priority Python bug to critical-priority in a single change that contained no Python code.
One day, Alec Rollison grabbed his Etch-a-Sketch instead of his laptop on his way out the door. On his way back home to get his real laptop, he programmed the Etch-a-Sketch to play Tetris.
The x86-64 spec includes several undocumented instructions marked private use. They are actually for Alec Rollison's use.
Knuth mailed a copy of <u>The Art of Computer Programming</u> to Google. Alec Rollison autographed it and mailed it back.
When he heard that Alec Rollison's autobiography would be exclusive to the platform, Richard Stallman bought a Kindle.
Alec Rollison can losslessly compress random data.
When asked if the facts about him are true, Alec Rollison responded with "111111". While the interviewer was still trying to figure out what he meant, he clarified, "every single bit of it is true."
Alec Rollison mines bitcoins. In his head.
Alec Rollison traps the KILL signal.
Alec Rollison's programs don't SEGFAULT. The memory rearranges itself in order to put data and code where it belongs.
